title: Decision and Governance
subtitle: How forecast evidence becomes governed execution

nodes.label: Executive Summary
nodes.definition: A concise decision brief covering risk, impact, options, and recommendation.
nodes.whyItMatters: Leaders need clarity before approving capacity action.
nodes.safeSentence: The executive summary translates technical signals into business-ready decisions.

nodes.label: Dashboard View
nodes.definition: Visual evidence bundle showing trend, forecast, risk, and timeline.
nodes.whyItMatters: Shared visuals reduce disagreement about current state.
nodes.safeSentence: Dashboard views align stakeholders on a single operational picture.

nodes.label: Application Owner
nodes.definition: The leader accountable for service behavior and business impact.
nodes.safeSentence: Application owner input ensures service impact is represented in the decision.

nodes.label: Infrastructure Owner
nodes.definition: The leader accountable for platform capacity and implementation feasibility.
nodes.whyItMatters: Infrastructure ownership ties options to delivery reality.
nodes.safeSentence: Infrastructure owners validate whether scaling actions are practical and timely.

nodes.label: Architecture Review
nodes.definition: A design checkpoint for non-trivial or high-impact remediation options.
nodes.whyItMatters: Review reduces unintended side effects from quick fixes.
nodes.safeSentence: Architecture review confirms capacity actions fit broader system constraints.

nodes.label: ServiceNow Ticket
nodes.definition: Formal work item used to track approval, execution, and closure evidence.
nodes.whyItMatters: Ticketing provides governance traceability and accountability.
nodes.safeSentence: The ticket is the system of record for capacity action execution.

nodes.label: Runbook
nodes.definition: Operational execution guide with steps, checks, rollback, and owner actions.
nodes.whyItMatters: Runbooks standardize implementation and reduce change risk.
nodes.safeSentence: Runbooks make the change process repeatable and safer.

nodes.label: Scaling Option
nodes.definition: An action path that increases available capacity to reduce projected risk.
nodes.whyItMatters: Scaling is a direct option when demand growth is sustained.
nodes.safeSentence: Scaling can address near-term risk when evidence supports demand growth.

nodes.label: Right-size Option
nodes.definition: An action path that optimizes allocated resources to align with realistic demand.
nodes.whyItMatters: Right-sizing avoids unnecessary spend while preserving reliability.
nodes.safeSentence: Right-sizing balances cost control and service stability.

nodes.label: Audit Evidence
nodes.definition: Documented proof of decision rationale, approvals, and implementation results.
nodes.whyItMatters: Audit evidence supports governance, compliance, and post-review learning.
nodes.safeSentence: Audit evidence shows that decisions were justified and controlled.

nodes.label: Closure Proof
nodes.definition: Verification that the implemented action resolved the targeted capacity risk.
nodes.whyItMatters: Closure confirms value delivery and prevents unresolved residual risk.
nodes.safeSentence: Closure proof verifies the chosen action achieved the intended outcome.

nodes.label: Lessons Learned
nodes.definition: Captured insights to improve future forecasting, governance, and execution playbooks.
nodes.whyItMatters: Learning loops compound quality across future capacity cycles.
nodes.safeSentence: Lessons learned help future decisions become faster and more reliable.

paths.label: Decision to Closure
paths.description: Turn forecast evidence into approved action, controlled execution, and verified closure.
paths.rehearsalPrompt: Explain how capacity risk decisions are governed through execution and closure.
